message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): build_tls_bundle failure-path contracts (tick 333)

Added three narrow tests pinning that `build_tls_bundle` returns the
right `DistributedTlsError` variant under each failure condition:

- `build_tls_bundle_rejects_tls_disabled_config` → `TlsDisabled`
- `build_tls_bundle_rejects_missing_cert_path` → `MissingCertPath`
- `build_tls_bundle_rejects_missing_key_path` → `MissingKeyPath`

The existing `tls_error_display_variants` test proves each enum
variant has a reasonable operator-facing message, but did NOT prove
that `build_tls_bundle` actually produces those specific variants
under the relevant failure conditions. A regression that quietly
swapped `MissingCertPath` for `Config("missing cert")` (or vice
versa) would still produce a reasonable message but would break
automated matching on the error variant (e.g. retry vs. config-error
routing at caller sites).

Together these give variant-level pinning for the three most common
mis-config scenarios operators hit when wiring mTLS for distributed
mode. The `MissingClientCaPath` variant (mTLS + no client CA) is
also uncovered but requires `auth_mode.requires_mtls()` true, which
would make the test config heavier; the three most common paths are
the high-value slice for this tick.

Uses explicit match + panic pattern rather than `.expect_err()`
because `DistributedTlsBundle` does not implement Debug (it holds
Arc<ServerConfig> + Arc<ClientConfig> from rustls, neither of which
impl Debug by default).

Test result: 3 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 18s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
